items.title: Enhance ACE Token Benefits Documentation in Docs 📚
items.summary: We've added a comprehensive guide on $ACE Token Benefits to our documentation site, available in both Chinese and English. This includes details on what $ACE is, discount tiers based on holdings, a step-by-step buying guide, and an FAQ section. The new pages are now part of the 'Getting Started' group for easy navigation.

items.title: Enhance error handling by sharing skip patterns in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates skip patterns into a single source of truth in repair_record.py, improving error handling in both the scheduler and HybridPublisher. The HybridPublisher now checks these patterns against error messages and page URLs before triggering repair issues, effectively reducing false positives related to non-code-bug errors like cookie expiry and rate limits.

items.title: Automated i18n translations updated from Chinese source in Frontend Web 🌐
items.summary: This update includes auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation changes derived from the Chinese (zh-CN) source files. Multiple language JSON files such as Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese were modified to improve translation accuracy and consistency. These changes enhance multilingual support and user experience across the platform frontend.

items.title: Boost Your Savings with ACE Token Promotion in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new carousel slide on the homepage promoting the benefits of the $ACE token, featuring calls to action for wallet binding and purchasing. Additionally, a dismissible banner on the console dashboard provides contextual messaging based on the user's wallet state, encouraging engagement with the ACE token. The changes also include internationalization updates for both English and Chinese languages.

items.title: Revamp Platform Rank Page UI with New Icons and Design in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the Platform Rank page by improving the UI elements, including the stat card icons and podium designs. The top 3 podium cards now feature crown icons instead of medals, and the ranking numbers are more visible with a new method for displaying ranks. Additionally, the FontAwesome library has been updated to include the crown icon for better visual representation.

items.title: Standardize SDK Design for AceDataCloud in Index 🚀
items.summary: We've added the Phase 1 SDK design RFC to outline cross-language conventions for Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs. This document establishes key structures, options, and error handling to ensure consistency across implementations. With this freeze, we are set to move forward into the next phase of development.

items.title: Enhance CI with Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Nano Banana 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CI job that triggers a Dify plugin sync whenever a publish event occurs on PyPI. The workflow modification ensures that the Dify plugin is notified of updates, enhancing integration and deployment processes. Key changes include the addition of a new step in the publish workflow to handle the dispatch.

items.title: Enhance CI with Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Seedream 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CI job that notifies the Dify plugin of updates upon publishing to PyPI. The workflow modification includes a POST request to trigger the Dify plugin sync, ensuring that the latest changes are reflected immediately. This enhancement streamlines the integration process and improves the overall deployment workflow.

items.title: Enhance Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Seedance 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new workflow step to notify the Dify plugin upon publishing to PyPI. The addition of the 'notify-dify-plugin' job ensures that the Dify plugin is synchronized with the latest updates, enhancing integration and user experience.

items.title: Enhance Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Flux 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CI job that triggers a Dify plugin sync whenever a PyPI publish occurs. The workflow modification ensures that the Dify plugin is notified of updates, enhancing integration and responsiveness. Key changes include the addition of a notification step in the publish workflow.

items.title: Enhance CI with Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Veo 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CI job that triggers a Dify plugin sync whenever a publish event occurs on PyPI. The workflow modification includes a POST request to the Dify repository to notify about the plugin update, ensuring that the latest changes are reflected promptly. This enhancement improves the integration between the MCPVeo project and the Dify plugin ecosystem.

items.title: Enhance Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Sora 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new workflow step to notify the Dify plugin of updates upon publishing to PyPI. The addition of the 'notify-dify-plugin' job ensures that the Dify plugin syncs with the latest changes, enhancing integration and responsiveness. This change aims to streamline the update process for users relying on the Dify plugin.

items.title: Enhance CI with Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Luma 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CI job that triggers a Dify plugin sync whenever a PyPI publish occurs. The workflow modification ensures that the Dify plugin receives the latest updates seamlessly, improving integration and deployment efficiency.

items.title: Automate Dify plugin sync on MCP publish in M C P Midjourney 🚀
items.summary: Added a new GitHub Actions job to automatically dispatch a 'mcp-plugin-update' event to the Dify repository whenever the MCP server is published to PyPI. This triggers the plugin version synchronization in the Dify repo, following the existing pattern used for notifying the VSCode extension rebuild. The change enhances automation and ensures plugin versions stay up to date without manual intervention.

items.title: Automate Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Serp 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new workflow that automatically triggers a `mcp-plugin-update` event to the Dify repository whenever the MCP server is published to PyPI. This enhancement ensures that the plugin versions remain in sync, improving the overall integration process. The changes were made in the GitHub Actions workflow file.

items.title: Automate Dify Plugin Sync on Publish in M C P Suno 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an automated dispatch of the `mcp-plugin-update` event to the Dify repository whenever the MCP server is published to PyPI. The new job, `notify-dify-plugin`, follows the existing pattern used for notifying the VSCode extension, ensuring seamless synchronization of plugin versions. This enhancement streamlines the update process and improves integration between the projects.

items.title: Enhance CI with Backend Model Sync Workflow in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new CI workflow that triggers on backend model changes, allowing for automatic issue creation for model synchronization. It facilitates the comparison and syncing of seven unmanaged mirror models, streamlining the development process. The workflow is designed to respond to specific dispatch events from the PlatformBackend.

items.title: Automate Model Change Notifications in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GitHub Action that triggers a backend-model-change event to PlatformGateway whenever model files or migrations are modified on the main branch. The addition of the notify-gateway workflow ensures that PlatformGateway can keep its unmanaged mirror models in sync with the latest changes, enhancing overall system reliability.

items.title: Enhance Error Detection with Automated Fixes in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a daily scheduled workflow that detects high-frequency 5xx errors from Tencent Cloud CLS logs. It aggregates errors by API path and status code, and automatically creates GitHub issues for investigation. Key files added include a Python script for error aggregation and a GitHub Actions workflow for automation.

items.title: Enhance MCP Plugin Sync Workflow in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new workflow, sync-mcp-plugins.yml, designed to handle repository_dispatch events triggered by MCP servers upon PyPI publish. It automates the creation of issues for Claude to update the relevant Dify plugins, streamlining the plugin management process.

items.title: Enhance Repair Pipeline Functionality in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the HybridPublisher by triggering the repair pipeline on failure and including screenshots from AI repair attempts in GitHub Issues. Additionally, it introduces skip patterns to prevent unnecessary repair triggers and adds essential environment variables for deployment in Kubernetes. These changes aim to streamline the publishing process and enhance error handling.

items.title: Enhance Profile Update Triggers in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update expands the organization profile update workflow to trigger immediately upon changes in Skills, Docs, Nexior, PlatformFrontend, and Dify surface repositories. Previously, these updates would wait for a scheduled fallback, improving responsiveness and ensuring timely profile generation.

items.title: Add Skills and agent entrypoints to organization profile in .github 🚀
items.summary: Updated the organization profile generator to include the new Skills repository and agent-facing entrypoints. The profile/README.md was regenerated from the script to ensure the org profile remains sourced from generate.py instead of manual edits. This enhances visibility of key repositories and streamlines profile maintenance.

items.title: Kickstarting SDK Phase 0 Planning in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the main phased SDK rollout plan and a machine-readable Phase 0 scope file for SDK families and endpoints. It establishes a structured PR workflow for the SDK effort, focusing on contract preparation and scope freeze. The groundwork laid here will facilitate the next phases of SDK development across multiple programming languages.

items.title: Enhance SEO with GEO metadata and llms.txt support in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces comprehensive route-level SEO metadata utilities for Nexior, including dynamic titles, descriptions, keywords, canonical URLs, OG/Twitter tags, hreflang links, and JSON-LD schemas for WebApplication and Organization. Additionally, a new llms.txt file and references in robots.txt were added to improve GEO discovery by AI search engines. The previously used prerender integration has been removed in this release.

items.title: Enhance Nginx Performance and Security in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several key improvements to Nginx configuration, including enabling `gzip_static` for serving pre-compressed files and adding essential security headers. Additionally, the initialization process has been optimized by running user, site, and config initializations in parallel, reducing app boot time by approximately 150-300ms.

items.title: Boosting Security and Performance in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Nginx configuration for auth.acedata.cloud by enabling gzip_static for serving pre-compressed files and implementing critical security headers. Additionally, the Dockerfile has been updated to upgrade Node from version 16 to 20, aligning with the latest standards and ensuring better performance. These changes collectively improve both security and efficiency.

items.title: Boosted nginx performance and security with gzip_static and headers in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the nginx configuration for platform.acedata.cloud by enabling gzip_static to serve pre-compressed files, reducing CPU load. It also disables server tokens to hide nginx version information and adds key security headers to prevent MIME-type sniffing, clickjacking, and limit referrer leakage. These changes improve both performance and security of the frontend web server.

items.title: Streamline Repair Pipeline with Screenshot Capture in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly simplifies the repair pipeline by removing the Claude Code CLI mode and focusing on GitHub Issue creation with full diagnostics. It introduces screenshot capture for browser publish failures, enhancing error reporting. Key changes include the integration of screenshot uploads via the Platform API and adjustments to the repair record handling.

items.title: Boosting Frontend Performance with Optimizations in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant frontend loading performance enhancements for Nexior. Key changes include enabling gzip compression, implementing caching strategies for static assets, and deferring the loading of Solana wallet adapters to improve initial load times. Additionally, bundle splitting has been optimized to enhance long-term cacheability.

items.title: Boosting Frontend Performance with Optimizations in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant frontend loading performance enhancements for auth.acedata.cloud. Key changes include enabling gzip compression for various file types, implementing caching strategies for static assets, and optimizing resource hints for improved connectivity. Additionally, bundle splitting has been refined to enhance cacheability and reduce load times.

items.title: Boosting Frontend Performance with Optimizations in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant performance enhancements to the frontend by implementing gzip compression, immutable caching, and lazy loading of Web3 dependencies. Key metrics show a reduction in initial JavaScript size by 79%, leading to faster load times and improved user experience. The changes also include preloading critical resources and optimizing the Nginx configuration for better asset delivery.

items.title: Enhance Coin Policy Automation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update merges the functionality of the standalone `populate_coin_policies.py` into `coin.py`, enabling automatic population of coin policies during the existing CronJob that runs every 10 minutes. A new function, `ensure_coin_policies()`, has been added to create a standard 6-tier discount structure for any Api-type service lacking policies, while also ensuring idempotency by skipping services that already have them. The standalone script has been removed as it is no longer necessary.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces automated updates for internationalization (i18n) translations sourced from zh-CN. Key modifications include updates to multiple language files such as Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese, enhancing the localization support across the platform.

items.title: Revamp Channel Configuration with Backend Schema in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new backend-driven channel configuration dialog, replacing the previous hardcoded system. The `ConfigureChannelDialog.vue` component now dynamically renders channel-specific text directly from the backend, enhancing localization and user experience. Additionally, the `Channels.vue` file has been streamlined for better usability and clarity.

items.title: Enhanced Publish Channel Config Translations in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces backend-driven publish channel configuration translations with internationalization (i18n) support. A new migration creates translation records for 20 channels in both Chinese and English, while the serializer now resolves translation keys based on the request's Accept-Language header. These changes improve user experience by providing localized content for different languages.

items.title: Enhance Coin Policies for All Services in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new script, `populate_coin_policies.py`, designed to establish a standardized 6-tier discount structure for all API-type services. Previously, only ChatDoc and Claude AI had CoinPolicy records, but this script will now populate the same tier structure across all active services. The implementation will require running the script in production post-merge to activate the new policies.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automated translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to multiple language files, enhancing the user experience for various locales. The changes ensure that users across different languages receive consistent and accurate translations.

items.title: Streamline Coin Page Layout in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the coin page by removing duplicate introduction text and adjusting the wallet section title to 'My Wallet'. Additionally, the redundant Wallet Bound status badge has been eliminated for a cleaner look. New internationalization keys have also been added for both English and Chinese versions.

items.title: Enhance Ranking Page with Colorful Avatars and Podium Effects in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update revamps the ranking page by introducing Teams-inspired colorful avatars and distinct gold, silver, and bronze podium distinctions. Key changes include a new 14-color palette for avatar backgrounds, larger medal icons, and improved hover animations for podium cards. Additionally, the avatar display now features 2-character initials for non-photo users, enhancing visual clarity and engagement.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to various language files, enhancing the user experience for multiple languages. The changes ensure that the application remains accessible and user-friendly across different regions.

items.title: Enhance API with CSV Export Feature in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GET endpoint at /api/v1/usage/apis/export/ that allows users to export API usage records in CSV format. The implementation includes modifications to the URL routing and the addition of a dedicated view for handling the CSV export functionality. This enhancement will improve data accessibility for users analyzing API usage.

items.title: Add CSV Export Button to Usage Page in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an Export CSV button on the usage page, allowing users to download filtered usage records as a CSV file. The feature utilizes the new backend endpoint for exporting data, aligning with the existing order export functionality. Key modifications include updates to the UI and localization files for both English and Chinese.

items.title: Enhance Repair Pipeline Integration in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the AI auto-repair pipeline into the main publish scheduler, ensuring it triggers for all publisher types, including ApiPublisher and HybridPublisher. Key changes include the addition of a failure recording function and enhanced prompts with traceback support for better debugging. This improvement streamlines the error handling process, automatically creating GitHub issues and facilitating code fixes through Claude Code Action.

items.title: Enhance OAuth2 Experience with User Info and Dark Mode in Frontend Web 🌟
items.summary: This update improves the OAuth2 authorization page by fixing the login redirect for expired tokens, ensuring users are redirected to login when necessary. It also displays current user information, including avatar and email, on the consent page, enhancing user awareness. Additionally, dark mode styling has been refined for better visual consistency across themes.

items.title: Organize Publisher Docs for Better Access in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The publisher documentation has been successfully relocated from the root directory to the new 'docs/zh-CN/' folder. This change includes five key files that enhance the organization and accessibility of the documentation for users. Overall, this update aims to streamline navigation and improve user experience.

items.title: Upgrade slugify to v3 with Node.js 20 support in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps @sindresorhus/slugify from version 2.2.1 to 3.0.0, introducing a requirement for Node.js 20. New options for transliteration and locale have been added, enhancing slug generation capabilities. The upgrade ensures compatibility with the latest Node.js environment and improves text processing features.

items.title: Upgrade FingerprintJS to Version 5.1.0 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the FingerprintJS library from version 4.5.1 to 5.1.0, introducing several improvements including stable fingerprints in Firefox across normal and incognito modes. Additionally, the TypeScript compilation target has been updated to ES2018, which may affect compatibility with older environments. The project license has also changed to MIT, enhancing its permissiveness for community contributions.
